mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Spearman correlation between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor-market deterioration and impending demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of cases or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate above 2.0 percent and an unemployment rate below 4.5 percent indicates restrictive policy hitting a tight labor market, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE shows no significantly negative predictive coefficient (p > 0.05) for 20-day SPX returns in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low unemployment, month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5 percent, RSAFS changes show no positive Spearman correlation with 20-day SPX returns, or the conditional mean return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Changes in FEDFUNDS are not positively correlated with 5-day SPX returns, with walk-forward rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its prior three-month average signals a manufacturing contraction that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month trailing average
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX forward returns in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 2.0 percent while CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, the interaction of tight monetary policy and above-target inflation raises real discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative 60-day returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce negative average 60-day SPX returns, or a univariate model with either proxy alone achieves comparable or better predictive R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month job growth as measured by nonfarm payrolls signals household income expansion and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS changes are not positively correlated with 5-day SPX returns, with walk-forward rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
